execution_blocker.details: v9 OOF runner (PID 55706) was killed at ~340/773 wells (likely OOM under contention from concurrent feature-build / LGB / kriging probe processes). Available memory hit 0 GB; load average peaked at 60 on 10-core M1 Pro. Both /tmp/v9_oof.csv and /tmp/v9_well_rmse.csv were never written. New multiseed runs deferred to a less-contested window.

stage1_lgb_multiseed.expected_runtime_when_uncontended: 75 min (25 min features + 50 min for 5 LGB seeds)

theoretical_estimate_at_full_v9_features.method: Variance reduction with seed-to-seed correlation rho ~ 0.6-0.75 for LGB
theoretical_estimate_at_full_v9_features.rationale: Tail wells have high seed variance because they hinge on a small number of trees. Var reduction from N-seed avg = (1 + (N-1)*rho)/N. With rho=0.7 and N=5, factor = 0.76 in variance, ~13% reduction in RMSE. Catastrophic-tail individual cases can see much larger reductions.

stage2_mlp_multiseed_at_imputer_level.comparison_basis: 5-fold GroupKFold OOF over 765 wells / 3,746,966 rows; MLP+PE-L8 multi-output predicting all 6 formation tops
stage2_mlp_multiseed_at_imputer_level.interpretation: MLP multi-seed gives substantial improvement on the worst well (-18ft on the 165-ft outlier) but is ROI-NEGATIVE on 4/11 catastrophic-tail wells. Net improvement ~ -0.49 RMSE overall but very high noise on the tail. The 11 wells > 60ft remain 11; multi-seed MLP shifts the distribution slightly, doesn't fix the underlying spatial-isolation/regime-change problems in those wells.
stage2_mlp_multiseed_at_imputer_level.compute_cost: ~3x MLP fit time per fold (~45s -> ~135s wallclock per fold)
stage2_mlp_multiseed_at_imputer_level.verdict_for_stage2: Marginal at the imputer level. The robust path for Stage 2 is to run multi-seed-MLP features through the GBM stack and see if the GBM amplifies or dampens the imputer-level improvement. NOT YET MEASURED at the GBM stack level.

recommendation.rationale: Production v9 should ship with 5 LGB seeds [42, 7, 123, 999, 31337]. With seed-to-seed correlation ~0.7, the marginal benefit beyond 5 seeds is small (3rd seed adds ~6% variance reduction, 5th adds ~3%, 8th adds ~1.5%). LGB is fast enough that 5x cost on the GBM stage is fine. Use simple averaging in OOF space (model-blind), then re-fit each seed on full train for inference.
recommendation.rationale_mlp: MLP multi-seed gives only -0.49 RMSE overall and is highly noisy on the tail (4/11 catastrophic wells got worse). Compute cost is 3x. Skip MLP multi-seed unless you can use 5+ MLPs (impractical at 250s each).
recommendation.alternative_seeds_strategy: Per the user's HFT-mechanical-sympathy thesis, vary OTHER axes of randomness instead: (a) bootstrap-resample wells across the 5 GBMs, (b) vary GroupKFold split seed (already varying with random_state=seed), (c) train models on disjoint feature subsets (boosting redundancy fights catastrophic outliers). Multi-seed is the cheapest first move; the decorrelation moves above are the next step if multi-seed delta < 0.2.
